title: Scale CloudNative-PG cluster to 3 replicas with automated failover
subtasks: Edit the CloudNative-PG Cluster CR for 'sigma-1-pg' to set spec.instances: 3.
Ensure replication and failover settings are enabled for automated promotion.
Apply the updated CR and verify all 3 pods reach Ready state.
Check status.instances and status.readyInstances both equal 3.
Simulate a primary pod failure and verify a new primary is elected within 30 seconds.
Validate streaming replication by connecting to a replica and running SELECT pg_is_in_recovery();

title: Configure Redis HA with Sentinel and update ConfigMap connection string
subtasks: Determine if the current Redis deployment supports Sentinel/replicated mode; if not, deploy a Redis Sentinel StatefulSet (3 Redis nodes + 3 Sentinel nodes) using a supported Helm chart.
Configure Sentinel to monitor the master named 'sigma-1-redis' with quorum 2.
Update the sigma-1-infra-endpoints ConfigMap to use a Sentinel-aware connection string (e.g., redis-sentinel://sentinel-0:26379,sentinel-1:26379,sentinel-2:26379/sigma-1-redis).
Apply ConfigMap changes and ensure all consumers pick up the new endpoint.
Validate Sentinel is monitoring the master and can promote a replica on failure.

title: Configure Ingress with TLS termination via cert-manager for PM server
subtasks: Install cert-manager in the cluster if not already present.
Create a ClusterIssuer or Issuer for Let's Encrypt (staging for test, production for go-live).
Create an Ingress resource for the PM server with TLS enabled and cert-manager annotations.
Add ingress annotations for rate limiting and request size limits.
Apply the Ingress and Certificate resources and wait for the certificate to become Ready.
Verify TLS is working by accessing the PM server endpoint with curl/openssl.

title: Harden Cilium NetworkPolicies with deny-all default and exact port allowlisting
subtasks: Create a default deny-all CiliumNetworkPolicy for the sigma-1-dev namespace.
Author allowlist policies for PM server ingress (from ingress controller pods on TCP 3000).
Allow PM server egress to CloudNative-PG (TCP 5432), Redis (TCP 6379/26379), bridge services, and external APIs (Linear, GitHub, Hermes) using toFQDNs or toCIDRSet.
Allow DNS egress to kube-dns (UDP 53).
Allow CloudNative-PG replication traffic between PG pods (TCP 5432).
Allow Redis Sentinel inter-node traffic (TCP 6379, 26379).
Apply all policies and verify legitimate connectivity is preserved and unauthorized traffic is blocked.

title: Audit and tighten RBAC with dedicated Roles for all service accounts
subtasks: Audit all RoleBindings and ClusterRoleBindings in sigma-1-dev for wildcard or cluster-admin permissions.
Remove or replace any overly permissive bindings.
Create a dedicated Role for the PM server service account (sigma-1-pm-sa) with only get/list/watch on ConfigMaps and Secrets.
Create RoleBindings for each service account with least-privilege permissions.
Ensure no service account has create/delete pods or cluster-wide write permissions.
Verify permissions using kubectl auth can-i for each service account.

title: Configure secret rotation via external-secrets with refreshInterval and rolling restart triggers
subtasks: Update all ExternalSecret CRDs in sigma-1-dev to include spec.refreshInterval: 1h.
For pods mounting secrets as env vars, configure rolling restart triggers (install and annotate with Reloader or use a hash annotation referencing the Secret's resourceVersion).
Apply all changes and verify the external-secrets operator reconciles on schedule.
Test secret rotation by updating a value in the backing secret store and confirming the Kubernetes Secret updates and pods restart as needed.

title: Add audit logging for namespace security events
subtasks: Determine if cluster-level audit policy can be modified; if so, add a namespace-scoped audit rule for sigma-1-dev to capture secret access events.
If not possible, deploy a structured logging sidecar (e.g., Falco or kube-audit-rest) in the namespace to capture secret access and RBAC denials.
Ensure audit logs are shipped to the cluster's logging backend or accessible via kubectl logs.
Test audit logging by performing a secret access and verifying the event is captured in the audit logs.

title: Document all production hardening changes in docs/production-hardening.md
subtasks: Create docs/production-hardening.md with sections for each hardening measure: CloudNative-PG HA, Redis HA/Sentinel, Ingress & TLS, Cilium Network Policies, RBAC, Secret Rotation, Audit Logging.
For each section, include rationale, configuration summary (YAML snippets or manifest references), and verification commands.
Add a summary table at the top listing all measures and their status.
Ensure the document is at least 500 words and covers at least 6 distinct hardening measures.
Commit the documentation alongside the infrastructure changes.
